packageRules.description: darkhttpd is pinned by version AND a tarball content hash (DARKHTTPD_SHA256) that Renovate cannot recompute on its own: the inherited github-tags manager discovers the version, but github-tags only exposes the git commit sha, not the sha256 of the auto-generated archive that `sha256sum -c` verifies, and the hosted Renovate app does not run postUpgradeTasks. So a version-bump PR carries a stale SHA and fails the integrity check until a human updates it. Label the PR and ship the exact recompute command in its body so the residual manual step is one copy-paste rather than a hunt. Automerge is intentionally left inherited (on): once the SHA is corrected and the build goes green, the PR merges on its own.
prBodyNotes:
:warning: **Manual step required — update `DARKHTTPD_SHA256`.** This bump changes `DARKHTTPD_VERSION` only. Renovate cannot recompute the tarball hash, so the build will fail `sha256sum -c` until you update `DARKHTTPD_SHA256` in the `Dockerfile` on this branch. Recompute it with:

```sh
curl -sL https://github.com/emikulic/darkhttpd/archive/refs/tags/{{{newValue}}}.tar.gz | sha256sum
```

Replace the `DARKHTTPD_SHA256` value with the printed hash and push to this branch; the PR will go green and auto-merge.
